description_long: This is a small, cylindrical white plastic bottle with a screw-top lid, commonly used for over-the-counter medications. The bottle features a prominent blue and yellow label on the front, clearly displaying the word 'ASPIRIN' in large yellow letters, along with dosage information and branding. The label wraps around the bottle, with one side showing a barcode and the other side listing drug facts and ingredients in fine print. The cap is slightly wider than the neck of the bottle and is currently unscrewed, revealing the threaded mouth. The bottle appears lightweight and is likely designed to hold solid tablets or pills. The overall appearance is clean, clinical, and typical of pharmaceutical packaging found in urban pharmacies or medicine cabinets.
description: A small white plastic bottle with a screw cap and a blue-yellow aspirin label, designed for holding tablets. The label includes dosage, branding, and drug facts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the barcode and part of the drug facts label, with the cap unscrewed and resting above the bottle.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main blue and yellow 'ASPIRIN' label, dosage information, and branding, with the open mouth and cap above.
description_view_2: Angled view highlighting the transition between the main label and the drug facts panel, with the cap unscrewed.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the detailed drug facts and ingredients panel, with the cap unscrewed and no branding visible.
attribute_label_text: ASPIRIN Internal Analgesic (NSAID) 325 mg Pain Reliever • Fever Reducer PharmaPureRx 300 TABLETS NDC 30998-158-04 [illegible drug facts and barcode text]
